title: Add calendar activation QA and apply step
description: Add a separate automation step for calendar activation after owner assignment, with availability and booking-rule QA.
acceptance_criteria: Activation refuses to run until owner assignment passes, availability/schedule is verified, booking notice/duration/interval/buffers are checked, and a free-slot smoke test passes; apply sends only the fields needed for activation.
notes: 2026-06-09: API activation proof completed for Liverpool Digital. Added activate-calendars command and applied it live: Consulta Gratuita (a2rL0hFhXf2sIdKZecoM) and On Site Visit (tWEirh80FGFIuCAIXGWb) now verify isActive=true with teamMembers unchanged. Remaining reason to keep bead open: availability/free-slot QA is not complete; both payloads report openHoursCount=0.

title: Prove or automate GHL website publication
description: Find a safe way to publish generated HTML/images into the correct GHL Website Home block, or document the browser/manual flow with proof selectors.
acceptance_criteria: QC proves generated HTML is present in the correct GHL page block and public URL, not just account_websites or location.website; landing_form remains gated until Twilio approval.
notes: 2026-06-09: Browser automation succeeded through authenticated Windows Chrome Profile 9. Saved generated HTML from https://www.getpatronpro.com/api/website/4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL into GHL Home custom-code element #custom-code-MTo38o_zdB via CodeMirror. Modal Save closed; builder Save showed Last saved Jun 09, 4:13 PM. Reload verification length=33116 sha256=94e6e0a2830dafaf69a87d76c5a3375fa1ce6f89dd8949527e155cfbb0be69cd with title/hero/landing_form/color markers present. Public preview https://api.getpatronpro.com/preview/JgrAMMXugg5Yi8QAnbDz returned 200 with hero/colors and no Custom HTML placeholder. Top-level Publish was not clicked; leave final publish/domain readiness for manual operator check because phone/email/domain/Twilio are still incomplete.

title: Productize Supabase and PatronPro panel onboarding state access
description: Add documented, secret-safe access for the PatronPro panel/Supabase onboarding state so the automation can read generated HTML, images, form submissions, contact IDs, status flags, and approval gates without copy/paste.
acceptance_criteria: QC can read the Liverpool Digital generated HTML/images and onboarding status from Supabase or panel APIs using env-based credentials; no secrets are written to artifacts; missing access fails as blocked with exact env names and remediation.
notes: 2026-06-09: Supabase follow-up steps from website publication session. Needed later for panel submissions/checklist/account approval/live docs, not for generated HTML read (public endpoint already works). Steps: 1) add or expose NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY via a secure local env file or 1Password item; 2) never paste service role key in chat/RLM/artifacts; 3) load env only in shell for harness runs; 4) run export-docs to read doc_pages; 5) run qc to hydrate account_submissions, account_checklist, account_websites, contact IDs, approved_at; 6) if panel API auth is preferred, provide an authenticated pp-session/browser session instead of service-role; 7) update QC artifacts and only mark checklist rows complete when evidence exists. Minimum verification: Supabase access row passes, latest Liverpool Digital account/submission is readable, docs export succeeds, no secrets appear in git diff or artifacts.

title: Confirm Brand Board create and update automation
description: Turn the current Brand Boards read finding into a safe automation step for applying client colors, with dry-run fixtures and explicit user confirmation before live Brand Board mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Automation can read existing Brand Boards, plan create/update payloads from generated or onboarding colors, apply only with explicit --apply, and QA proves the resulting main/accent/complementary colors match the selected palette.
notes: 2026-06-09: Brand Board create/update API proof completed for Liverpool Digital. Created Brand Board via POST /brand-boards/ with locationId, name, colors, default:true; then patched existing board via PATCH /brand-boards/{locationId}/{brandBoardId} to enforce default:true. Verification requires detail hydration because GET /brand-boards/{locationId} list can omit colors; detailed GET /brand-boards/{locationId}/{brandBoardId} verified default=true and colors #471F23, #F69309, #2F1417. Artifacts: brand-board-apply-2026-06-09.json (initial list-only verification gotcha), brand-board-default-apply-2026-06-09.json (final pass).

title: Resolve GHL contact form language and DND customization
description: Find a reliable automation path for the GHL Add Contact customize-form requirements: make Language required, remove DND Channels, and confirm the related setting that must be enabled in the same panel area.
acceptance_criteria: Either an official API/browser automation flow is implemented with QA screenshots/selectors, or the step is documented as manual with exact panel path, expected before/after state, and QC evidence requirements.

title: Assign Liverpool Digital calendar owner via GHL API
description: Apply the approved safe live mutation that assigns the single Liverpool Digital user as the responsible team member for the two onboarding calendars.
acceptance_criteria: Both target calendar IDs from booking custom values show exactly one teamMembers userId matching the single location user; calendar activation remains unchanged and inactive.
notes: Live GHL apply succeeded for location 4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL. Consulta Gratuita (a2rL0hFhXf2sIdKZecoM) and On Site Visit (tWEirh80FGFIuCAIXGWb) each verify teamMembers[].userId = 7f3lxWXzcFRp3QMYqotT. Both calendars remain inactive by design; activation is tracked separately in ppweb-1.

title: Liverpool Digital PatronPro GHL documentation automation
description: Create a working, scriptable system for location 4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL / Liverpool Digital by ingesting PatronPro panel documentation, extracting required setup changes, implementing dry-run-first automation, and adding a QC verifier. No live PatronPro panel, Supabase, or GHL mutation is allowed until the required-change list is documented and the user explicitly approves the run mode.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation is captured in RLM; required panel/GHL changes are listed before mutation; automation scripts support dry-run mode; QC verifies every documented setup step; FSN1-compatible run notes exist; final report is stored under dev/agents/artifacts and doc/reports; branch is committed and pushed.
notes: Epic completed for dry-run automation/QC system. Live writes remain intentionally out of scope until explicit approval and credentials are provided.

title: Ingest PatronPro panel documentation into RLM
description: Export or inspect available PatronPro panel documentation from repo and/or panel APIs, normalize it into local artifacts, then store the useful documentation index and summaries in RLM.
acceptance_criteria: RLM entries include currentness metadata, source paths, artifact role, and Liverpool Digital relevance; local documentation artifact records exact sources and commands used.
notes: Source-code-derived panel/GHL documentation index stored locally and in RLM. Live doc_pages export remains gated by missing Supabase env or authenticated panel session; export-docs command is implemented for that condition.

title: Extract Liverpool Digital required setup checklist
description: Translate the Patreon Pro/PatronPro panel documentation into a concrete list of setup changes for location 4cPIvLND9hFAIzWQ1ZbL before any live mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Checklist names every required field, custom value, calendar/domain/phone/email/landing/Stripe/client access step, whether it is API-scriptable, UI-only, or needs AI reasoning, and its QC proof.
notes: Required pre-change list created at dev/agents/artifacts/doc/plan/liverpool-digital-required-change-list-2026-06-09.md and stored in RLM.

title: Build dry-run automation scripts
description: Create scripts that can read and prepare GHL/panel updates for the documented steps, defaulting to dry-run and requiring explicit run-mode selection for writes.
acceptance_criteria: Scripts accept the Liverpool Digital location ID, use env-based credentials only, print planned operations without secrets, and fail closed when credentials or approval flags are missing.
notes: Dry-run automation harness implemented with qc, plan, and export-docs modes. No live mutation commands are implemented.

title: Build QC verifier for documented setup steps
description: Create a read-only QC harness that checks each documentation-derived setup step after automation or manual work.
acceptance_criteria: QC output maps every check to pass/fail/blocked, source evidence, endpoint or panel source, timestamp, and remediation note.
notes: QC verifier implemented and dry-run output generated under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/.

title: Prepare FSN1-compatible automation runbook
description: Document how the scripts can be run from FSN1 or tied into GHL automation without mixing this work into the separate FSN1 Slackbot branch.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook includes safe env paths, command examples, dry-run/write mode separation, logging destination, and rollback notes.
notes: FSN1 runbook and env template completed; runbook stored in RLM.

title: Produce final quality-loop report and handoff
description: Document each step performed, run the quality loop over the plan/artifacts, and produce a final report for Liverpool Digital automation.
acceptance_criteria: Report is saved under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/plan and copied to doc/reports/2026/06, and the final handoff includes bead IDs, verification, commit hash, push status, and RLM checkpoint.
notes: Quality-loop reviewer outputs, merge report, and finalized report completed and stored locally/RLM.

title: Browser automation fallback for GHL UI-only onboarding steps
description: After API-proven steps are exhausted, use a signed-in WSL browser profile with Playwright/Puppeteer to automate or QA GHL UI-only onboarding tasks such as Website editor publication, Contact Add form customization, DND Channels removal, platform language, and notification template settings.
acceptance_criteria: Run only after API attempts are documented; use an authenticated WSL browser profile; capture screenshots/selectors and before/after proof; do not bypass Twilio/Stripe/client gates; store successful patterns and failed gotchas in RLM.
notes: 2026-06-09: First browser fallback succeeded for Website editor. Authenticated Windows Chrome Profile 9 controlled through Windows Node CDP, not WSL localhost CDP. Proven selectors: builder frame page-builder.leadconnectorhq.com, #custom-code-MTo38o_zdB, button.btn-open-editor, #hl-builder-custom-code-modal .CodeMirror, #pg-website-builder__btn--save. Saved and reload-verified generated HTML; public preview returned 200 with markers. Remaining browser-fallback targets still open: Contact Add form Language/DND customization, platform language, notification templates, and any final publish/domain UI checks.
